tool_input.code:
from sklearn.preprocessing import OneHotEncoder, LabelEncoder
from sklearn.ensemble import RandomForestClassifier
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np

# Separate the features and the target
X = df.drop('attrition', axis=1)
y = df['attrition']

# Identify all categorical columns
categorical_columns = df.select_dtypes(include=['object']).columns

# Identify low and high cardinality columns
low_cardinality_cols = [col for col in categorical_columns if df[col].nunique() <= 5]
high_cardinality_cols = [col for col in categorical_columns if df[col].nunique() > 5]

# Apply one-hot encoding to low-cardinality columns
X_encoded = pd.get_dummies(X, columns=low_cardinality_cols)

# Apply label encoding to high-cardinality columns
label_encoders = {}
for col in high_cardinality_cols:
    le = LabelEncoder()
    X_encoded[col] = le.fit_transform(X_encoded[col])
    label_encoders[col] = le

# Train a Random Forest to compute feature importances
model = RandomForestClassifier(random_state=0)
model.fit(X_encoded, y)
feature_importances = model.feature_importances_

# Get the top 5 most important features
features = X_encoded.columns
important_features = sorted(zip(feature_importances, features), reverse=True)[:5]
important_features_names = [feature for _, feature in important_features]

# Check if the encoded dataset has more columns than the original
dataset_increase = len(X_encoded.columns) > len(X.columns)

print("Important Features:", important_features_names)
print("Dataset has more columns:", dataset_increase)

tool_input.code:
from sklearn.preprocessing import OneHotEncoder, LabelEncoder
from sklearn.ensemble import RandomForestClassifier
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np

# Separate the features and the target
X = df.drop('attrition', axis=1)
y = df['attrition']

# Identify all categorical columns
categorical_columns = df.select_dtypes(include=['object']).columns

# Identify low and high cardinality columns
low_cardinality_cols = [col for col in categorical_columns if df[col].nunique() <= 5]
high_cardinality_cols = [col for col in categorical_columns if df[col].nunique() > 5]

# Apply one-hot encoding to low-cardinality columns
X_encoded = pd.get_dummies(X, columns=low_cardinality_cols)

# Apply label encoding to high-cardinality columns
label_encoders = {}
for col in high_cardinality_cols:
    le = LabelEncoder()
    X_encoded[col] = le.fit_transform(X_encoded[col])
    label_encoders[col] = le

# Train a Random Forest to compute feature importances
model = RandomForestClassifier(random_state=0)
model.fit(X_encoded, y)
feature_importances = model.feature_importances_

# Get the top 5 most important features
features = X_encoded.columns
important_features = sorted(zip(feature_importances, features), reverse=True)[:5]
important_features_names = [feature for _, feature in important_features]

# Check if the encoded dataset has more columns than the original
dataset_increase = len(X_encoded.columns) > len(X.columns)

print("Important Features:", important_features_names)
print("Dataset has more columns:", dataset_increase)
